industries.name: Wood Preservation (Historical & Industrial)
industries.share: ~50% of arsenic use (declining rapidly in consumer markets)
uses: Chromated copper arsenate (CCA) was the dominant wood preservative for decades — pressure-treated lumber for decks, playgrounds, utility poles, and marine pilings. Banned for residential use in the US and EU by 2004 due to arsenic leaching concerns; still permitted for industrial applications

CCA-treated wood remains in service in millions of existing utility poles, railroad ties, and marine structures — long service life means legacy arsenic-treated wood persists in infrastructure for decades
Industrial CCA treatment still used for utility poles, highway guardrail posts, and agricultural fencing in many countries where residential restrictions don't apply
Alternative preservatives (copper azole, alkaline copper quaternary) have largely replaced CCA in consumer lumber but require more copper — driving increased copper demand in preservative treatment

companies.name: Koppers Holdings
companies.share: ~25% of North American treated wood market
companies.use: Major wood treatment company using copper-based (non-CCA) preservatives for railroad ties, utility poles, and structural lumber. Historically used CCA; now focused on creosote and copper-based alternatives for industrial wood treatment.

companies.name: Lonza Wood Protection (now Arxada)
companies.use: Major wood preservative chemical supplier. Produces copper azole (CA-B) and alkaline copper quaternary (ACQ) preservatives that replaced CCA in residential lumber. Supplies pressure-treating plants across North America, Europe, and Asia.

industries.name: Compound Semiconductors
uses: Arsenic (as arsine gas, AsH₃) is the n-type dopant in gallium arsenide (GaAs) compound semiconductor devices — GaAs RF chips, solar cells, and laser diodes all require precise arsenic incorporation during MOCVD epitaxial growth

Arsenic implantation as n-type dopant in silicon CMOS at 75As⁺ ion implantation — arsenic's low diffusivity in silicon makes it preferred over phosphorus for shallow source/drain junctions in sub-100nm transistors
Indium arsenide (InAs) for infrared photodetectors in the 1–3.5 μm range; InAs quantum dots for telecom wavelength lasers and single-photon sources in quantum computing research
Arsenic trisulfide (As₂S₃) and arsenic selenide (As₂Se₃) chalcogenide glasses for mid-infrared fiber optics transmitting 2–12 μm wavelengths — used in medical laser delivery and chemical sensing

companies.name: Umicore
companies.use: Produces high-purity arsenic metal and arsenic compounds for compound semiconductor and specialty chemical applications. Recovers arsenic from copper and lead smelting residues.

companies.name: 5N Plus
companies.use: Producer of high-purity (5N, 99.999%) arsenic metal and arsenic compounds for GaAs semiconductor, solar cell, and specialty chemical applications. Also produces InAs, GaAs, and other III-V compounds.

industries.name: Agrochemicals & Glass
uses: Monosodium methanearsonate (MSMA) organic arsenical herbicide: still registered for use on cotton, golf courses, and highway right-of-ways in the US after inorganic arsenicals were withdrawn — controls annual grasses and nutsedge

Arsenic trioxide (As₂O₃) in glass manufacturing as a fining agent — removes bubbles from molten glass by oxidizing and releasing oxygen at high temperature; used in optical glass, CRT glass, and specialty glass where clarity is paramount
Arsenic trioxide (Trisenox) as cancer therapeutic: FDA-approved for treatment of acute promyelocytic leukemia (APL) — induces differentiation and apoptosis in leukemic cells at therapeutic doses
Lead arsenate historically the primary insecticide in US apple and citrus orchards until replaced by organophosphates in the 1950s — legacy arsenic contamination remains in many former orchard soils

companies.name: Tronox Holdings
companies.use: Major producer of arsenic trioxide recovered as a byproduct of gold and copper smelting operations. Supplies As₂O₃ to glass manufacturers and specialty chemical producers.

companies.name: Teva Pharmaceutical
companies.use: Major producer of arsenic trioxide (Trisenox) pharmaceutical injection for treatment of relapsed/refractory acute promyelocytic leukemia (APL). An important niche medical use of an otherwise industrial chemical.
